subject: Gaming
title: Cooperation vs Competition
description: A 60-second lesson on Cooperation vs Competition.

hook.text: Co-op games make players kinder. Competitive games make players more aggressive. Design shapes behavior.

buildup.text: Research shows that cooperative gameplay increases prosocial behavior—even outside the game.

discovery.text: Games like Journey and It Takes Two create emotional bonds between strangers through shared goals.

twist.text: Competitive games increase cortisol and aggression short-term. But they also build resilience and focus.

climax.text: Neither is purely good or bad. The game's social structure shapes the player's psychological response.

punchline.text: Co-op builds empathy. Competition builds resilience. Design shapes us.

quiz.question: How does cooperative gameplay affect behavior?
options: It increases prosocial behavior, even outside the game
It has no effect on behavior
